title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
As Poirot and Carter hurry to catch a train, Carter expresses confusion regarding the reported attack on Margaret Ernst, questioning the motive if the previous suspects, Jane Hobbs and Samuel Kid, were not the killers. Poirot dismisses the idea that the case is solved, asserting that Jane Hobbs's account of the events is a carefully crafted lie.

Poirot re-examines the "redemption plan" involving Harriet Sipprell, Ada Gransbury, Richard Nigges, and Jane Hobbs, in which the participants intended to kill one another to atone for past sins. While analyzing a crossword puzzle clue for a six-letter word meaning "death," Poirot reaches a sudden realization regarding the truth of the murders.

Poirot argues that Jane Hobbs's version of events is factually impossible. He challenges the narrative that Richard Nigges committed suicide in a locked room after being persuaded by the others to take responsibility for the deaths of Patrick and Frances Ives. Poirot identifies a logical inconsistency regarding the hotel keys: if Jane Hobbs did not go to the Brawnsham Hotel as she claimed, it remains unclear how Samuel Kid obtained the keys to rooms 121 and 317 to plant them in Nancy Duane's pocket. Furthermore, Poirot notes that if Nigges had committed suicide by poison, he could not have meticulously arranged his own body into a straight, upright pose, suggesting the scene was staged by someone else.
